nodes.label: AI as boilerplate and learning accelerator
nodes.label_negative: Manual boilerplate and self-study without AI
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI most often to provide me boilerplate code, learn new programming concepts, and refresh on programming concepts

nodes.label: AI for tedious and repetitive tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI for high-attention and complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: saves me from doing repetitive tasks

nodes.label: AI delegation for secret information
nodes.grounding_span: Any tasks that involve secret information like API keys I do not delegate

nodes.label: AI for high-attention and complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Any tasks that require a high attention to detail like specific styling or implementing complex algorithms I prefer to do myself

nodes.label: AI as iterative problem-solving partner
nodes.grounding_span: I will often start with AI produced code and work on it until I run into a difficult problem

nodes.label: Peer influence on AI adoption
nodes.grounding_span: I was initially an AI naysayer but many of my colleagues were early adopters

nodes.label: AI reliability for algorithmic tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I found that it really struggled with drawing nodes and edges in their correct positions and could not make accurate distance calculations

nodes.label: AI skill delegation without concern
nodes.label_negative: Concern about skill atrophy due to AI
nodes.grounding_span: any skill that AI currently excel at I delegate completely

nodes.label: AI handling tedious programming to free up creativity
nodes.label_negative: AI encroaching on creative design work
nodes.grounding_span: I expect more and more of the tedious programming to be done by AI allowing me to focus on design and creative solutions

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: saves me from doing repetitive tasks

nodes.label: Security and control
nodes.grounding_span: Any tasks that involve secret information like API keys I do not delegate

nodes.label: Quality and precision
nodes.grounding_span: Any tasks that require a high attention to detail like specific styling or implementing complex algorithms I prefer to do myself

nodes.label: Creative autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: allowing me to focus on design and creative solutions

nodes.label: Positive adoption influenced by peers
nodes.grounding_span: seeing their productivity improve massively and hearing anecdotes about how much they enjoyed working with AI motivated me to incorporate it more

nodes.label: Frustration with AI on algorithmic tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I found that it really struggled with drawing nodes and edges in their correct positions and could not make accurate distance calculations

nodes.label: Optimistic expectation for AI handling tedious work
nodes.grounding_span: I expect more and more of the tedious programming to be done by AI allowing me to focus on design and creative solutions

nodes.label: Iterative problem-solving with resource switching
nodes.grounding_span: I will often start with AI produced code and work on it until I run into a difficult problem. I will use a combination of web searching and AI prompts to work through difficult problems.

nodes.label: Pragmatic delegation based on task characteristics
nodes.grounding_span: Any tasks that involve secret information like API keys I do not delegate. Any tasks that require a high attention to detail like specific styling or implementing complex algorithms I prefer to do myself.
